candidates.rationale: Current Google docs name Gemini 3.1 Pro preview and a custom-tools endpoint for agentic bash/custom-tool workflows; local Google Cloud project and services are available; the run can stay on the proven CodeClash path with a strict first-smoke budget.
OpenAI docs identify gpt-5.5 as the latest model for complex tool-heavy coding workflows, but this repo has no OPENAI_API_KEY in local env or GitHub secrets.
Pinned CodeClash already has Portkey-backed Google configs, but no PORTKEY_API_KEY is visible and the strongest current Google model would still require an updated config.
High-value target, but it adds environment and scoring complexity before the first paid provider receipt is proven on CodeClash.

criteria.artifact_surface: trajectory, metadata, diff-scope, logs, cost, and receipt can be audited
criteria.bounded_spend: max calls, tokens, wall time, and dollar ceiling can be frozen before execution
criteria.credential_readiness: secret-safe local or CI credential path exists without logging values
criteria.frontier_model_fit: current reasoning or agentic coding model verified from official docs
criteria.harness_fidelity: keeps the CodeClash Mini-SWE-Agent path proven in iter05-iter07
criteria.overclaim_control: pilot result cannot be mistaken for a leaderboard or production result
